title: Upon My Dear and Loving Husband his Going into England Jan. 16 by Anne Bradstreet — Love Poetry Lines
content:
O thou Most High who rulest all
And hear'st the prayers of thine,
O hearken, Lord, unto my suit
And my petition sign.

Into Thy everlasting arms Of mercy
I commend Thy servant, Lord.
Keep and preserve My husband,
meaning: "Upon My Dear and Loving Husband his Going into England Jan. 16" by Anne Bradstreet is a love poem consisting of 59 lines. The poet explores themes of love through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "O thou Most High who rulest all And hear'st the prayers of thine,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Bradstreet's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
